content: Ran this on a Samsung Galaxy S7 running Android 8.1.0/API 26 and only saw the error I mentioned [before](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/153075#issuecomment-2334629512).
I can reproduce this bug with Android API 35, it works just fine with API <= 34.
I solved it by adding **android.enableR8.fullMode=false** in **gradle.properties**.

> I can reproduce this bug with Android API 35, it works just fine with API <= 34.

I couldn't reproduce this error (only the one I saw [before](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/153075#issuecomment-2334629512)) with API 35 in debug or release mode.

In my case, it can be solved while I downgrade to com.android.tools.build:gradle:8.1.4.
Other versions (8.6.1, 8.5.2, 8.4.2, 8.3.2) will cause this error.

I have the same issue with others packages as described here : https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/154723#issuecomment-2369356508

Inspired by recent comments, I tested https://github.com/rekire/flutter_issue_153075 on a Pixel 7 API 35 emulator and only saw the same error that I reproduced [before](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/153075#issuecomment-2334629512) with a more specific error message.

@kuhnroyal @studiogaram or anyone else seeing the message currently, do you get the same error you are currently getting when running https://github.com/rekire/flutter_issue_153075/? Knowing this would help me deduce if there is an issue with my methods of reproduction or if there's other factors we're not considering.

Thanks @camsim99 for the repo.

I managed to reproduce the issue by adding all my app dependencies which directly failed.

I then removed them step by step, boiling it down to the `jni: 0.10.1` dependency.
As soon as this is part of the project, the shared preference initialization fails on an API 35 device.

Reproduction: https://github.com/kuhnroyal/flutter_issue_153075/commit/d479caced753f1b239e9090ccbb9fd0dbeb34e36

@HosseinYousefi You seem to be very active on the JNI front, do you have any insights?
If I update to `jni: 011.0` the app directly crashes.

I don't have enough context into this issue but it seems that it's an issue with SharedPreferences which does not use `package:jni`. Can you elaborate?

Sure, I can try to add my current train of thought.

The issue is surfacing as a SharedPreferences initialization problem on Android API 35 but I don't think this is the actual problem.

This only happens if `jni: 0.10.1` is added to the app, otherwise SharedPreferences works fine.

Since `shared_preferences` is not using `jni`, my assumption is that something during the `JniPlugin` registration is failing before the `SharedPreferencesPlugin` can be registered.

.... and after finishing that last thought I took a look at the Logcat output:

```
2024-09-26 16:02:08.141 15202-15202 GeneratedP...nsRegister eu....ssue153075.shared_prefs_issue  E  Tried to automatically register plugins with FlutterEngine (io.flutter.embedding.engine.FlutterEngine@3e9702c) but could not find or invoke the GeneratedPluginRegistrant.

So thats why I think this is actually a JNI problem on Android API 35.

> This only happens if `jni: 0.10.1` is added to the app, otherwise SharedPreferences works fine.

I see, is everyone else is having the same issue? I don't think that everyone here is depending on `package:jni`.

> .... and after finishing that last thought I took a look at the Logcat output:
> ```
> Caused by: java.lang.UnsatisfiedLinkError: dlopen failed: empty/missing DT_STRTAB in "/data/app/~~RVkrALZcww5pW8tkeVgAeg==/eu.rekisoft.flutter.issue153075.shared_prefs_issue-hPJ0FgN8X8hC2whkPKcVuA==/lib/arm64/libdartjni.so"
>```

This seems to be a problem with 16 KB page sizes, I opened an issue to reproduce and fix it: https://github.com/dart-lang/native/issues/1608

> If I update to `jni: 011.0` the app directly crashes.

Please open an issue with more details about the crash here: https://github.com/dart-lang/native
@reidbaker @gmackall You also might want to look into https://developer.android.com/guide/practices/page-sizes. Maybe it makes sense to increase the NDK version.
For me `jni` was pulled in via `native_dio_adapter` -> `cronet_http`.

These worked for me. I replaced
```dart
final prefs = await SharedPreferences.getInstance();
```
with
```dart
final prefs= await SharedPreferencesWithCache.create(
  cacheOptions: const SharedPreferencesWithCacheOptions(allowList: null),
);
```
and did a 
```bash
flutter clean
flutter pub get
```

for
```yaml
shared_preferences: ^2.3.2
```

Trying to summarize the reports here,

- This issue is not related to R8/minification nor debug vs release mode
- Replacing `final prefs = await SharedPreferences.getInstance()` with `await SharedPreferencesWithCache.create(...)` has worked for some and not all
- https://github.com/rekire/flutter_issue_153075 has reproduced the issue for some but not all (I have not been able to reproduce the issue with it)
- ITO my attempts to repro: adding a `final prefs = await SharedPreferences.getInstance()` call to a counter app did not reproduce it
- https://github.com/dart-lang/native/issues/1608 has appeared but seems unrelated to this issue

To move forward, a minimal reproduction would be super helpful here. I suspect there are differences within the reproductions that are yielding the inconsistent results.
The issue is not a proguard rules issue, as it happens in debug mode as well. Current fix is to downgrade to version 2.3.0.
I had run flutter pub clean severally with no success. I changed from emulator to physical device and it now runs.

Following  @Shunt22's comment above and @jacesimons14 , 
Using:
Dart SDK 3.6.8 and flutter 3.27.1.
Android SDK 35 (15)
Android Emulator and device both giving the same issue.
iOS platform using the same flutter code base is working as expected.

Creating fresh simple counter application  using SharedPreference works.
Using a application that was migrated from 2.19.1 to 3.27.1 is giving the following exception: 
PlatformException(channel-error, Unable to establish connection on channel: "dev.flutter.pigeon.shared_preferences_android.SharedPreferencesApi.getAll"., null, null)

Not sure if the migration missed something.
Really stuck on this one.

Further debugging indicated that when getString in shared_preferences_async.dart line 79 is called, the _dataStoreApi and _sharedPreferencesApi fields: pigeonVar_binaryMessenger is null. 

Is this perhaps an indication of the flutter channel not being setup correctly?
And how to remedy the situation?

Update for reference: 
Issue resolved when a dependency on another PlatForm channel was removed. 
I suspect that the removed implementation was using a fairly old pigeon package to create the channel.

> Update for reference: Issue resolved when a dependency on another PlatForm channel was removed. I suspect that the removed implementation was using a fairly old pigeon package to create the channel.

Hi @lampian It sounds that we have exactly the same problem (it surfaced when upgrading from 2.x version to 3.27.x. Can you be more specific on how you solved the problem, so that I could try repeat the same/similar steps?

Hi @laurisvan ,

In my case, after updating to flutter 3.27.x the issue surfaced. My application was using a custom plugin that somehow broke the method channels used in shared_preferences and other plugins dependent on method channels. 

Once I removed the offending plugin, shared_preferences and other started working again. 

The offending plugin was using outdated flutter version, gradle and pigeons. 

Once that was corrected, the offending plugin could be added back to the project without affecting shared_preference and the ithers that failed. 

Hope the approach could help you in identifying the source of the exception.

@stuartmorgan That's my fault.
But finally, I have fixed it, I created new project, move my code from old from to the new one and it works.

Open android project of flutter project in new tab in android studio.
then go to app's `MainActivity.class`

past below code in `MainActivity.class`

```
  override fun configureFlutterEngine(flutterEngine: FlutterEngine) {
        super.configureFlutterEngine(flutterEngine)

        flutterEngine.plugins.add(SharedPreferencesPlugin())

    }
```

This is what worked for me:

in `MainActivity`:

```
override fun configureFlutterEngine(flutterEngine: FlutterEngine) {
        super.configureFlutterEngine(flutterEngine)

        flutterEngine.plugins.add(LegacySharedPreferencesPlugin())

    }
```

just add `Legacy` to SharedPreferences.

Hi all, hope that I'm not too late.

Initial configuration:
Kotlin: 2.1.20
Flutter SDK 3.29.3
Dart SDK 3.7.2
AGP: 8.9.0

So, after one painful day of debugging here are my findings, based on some of the inputs here and my own:

It seems like the issue is not only related to shared_preferences, other plugins suffer as well, for example, I also had problems with
the permission_handler, but only in release mode.

For the shared_preferences, I have tried different configurations. Firstly, no matter if I use SharedPreferences,  SharedPreferencesAsync or SharedPreferencesWithCache, the result is the same.

Unfortunatelly, 

minifyEnabled false // this
shrinkResources false // this

didn't solve anything. Tried a variety of different **proguard rules,** the result was always the same.

The only thing that worked for me was lowering AGP to 8.3.2 as @OneXeor suggested. This triggered a diffrent chain of problems for
me => Kotlin version related, but the important part is that shared shared_preferences and permission_handler began working once again.

After switching to 8.4, everything fell apart once again. I took a quick glimpse at the release notes of [AGP 8.9.1](https://developer.android.com/build/releases/gradle-plugin) and they had fixed a really interesting [issue](https://issuetracker.google.com/issues/389737060) in it, not sure if this could be related though.


**Update:**

After returning eveything at the state of the beginning and adding:

import io.flutter.embedding.android.FlutterFragmentActivity
import io.flutter.embedding.engine.FlutterEngine
import io.flutter.plugins.sharedpreferences.SharedPreferencesPlugin

class MainActivity : FlutterFragmentActivity() {
  override fun configureFlutterEngine(flutterEngine: FlutterEngine) {
        super.configureFlutterEngine(flutterEngine)
        flutterEngine.plugins.add(SharedPreferencesPlugin())
    }
}

In the main activity, shared_preferences work, but now I see a lot of other errors such:
[ERROR:flutter/runtime/dart_vm_initializer.cc(40)] Unhandled Exception: MissingPluginException(No implementation found for method checkPermissionStatus on channel flutter.baseflow.com/permissions/methods)
[ERROR:flutter/runtime/dart_vm_initializer.cc(40)] Unhandled Exception: PlatformException(channel-error, Unable to establish connection on channel: "dev.flutter.pigeon.path_provider_android.PathProviderApi.getApplicationSupportPath"., null, null)

**Update 2: Managed to fire it up only with proguard rules.**

So, one of the solutions that I found was to add each plugin like in "Update 1". After reading this [stack](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/76800185/how-to-keep-some-com-google-android-play-core-classes-reported-missing-by-r8-d) I have created 3 super simple proguard rules taht have fixed my issue, without the need of modifying the MainActivity. Steps that I have followed:

1. Crete proguard-rules.pro file in the android/app dir
2. Add the needed keeps, in it in my case:
      -keep class com.baseflow.permissionhandler.** { *; }
      -keep class io.flutter.plugins.pathprovider.** { *; }
      -keep class io.flutter.plugins.sharedpreferences.** { *; }

3. In your build.gradle under release add:
     minifyEnabled true
     shrinkResources true
     proguardFiles getDefaultProguardFile('proguard-android-optimize.txt'), 'proguard-rules.pro'

Note: During my testing I have changed my SharedPreferences to SharedPreferencesAsync, but I think this shouldn't matter.

Hope, this blob of text would help someone.

I'm also receiving this error on iOS when I try to use Shared Preferences. In my case, I have a HomeWidget that's trying to invoke the app via a deeplink, goes through my `vm:entry-point`, but errors out when trying to send the method channel message

```
PlatformException(channel-error, Unable to establish connection on channel: "dev.flutter.pigeon.shared_preferences_foundation.UserDefaultsApi.set"., null, null)
```

The good news is, when I used SharedPreferences normally in my app, it works. It just doesn't seem to work when entering the app through a headless vm entry point

EDIT: I was able to get it all working by having a Receive Port open on my main entry point and having the vm:entry-point function send a message over the SendPort to execute the main chunk of code (with Shared preferences usage)

i still face this issue in debug mode it's working fine but in release mode shared_preferences: ^2.5.3 as well as permission_handler: ^12.0.0+1 not work I tried @Lucho3 all methods even though it's not working

In the `health_stats` project, your `example/ios/Podfile` appears to be almost full day older than `example/ios/Podfile.lock`. Perhaps the former has been copied from somewhere? I believe the consequence of a `Podfile` older than `Podfile.lock` is that `pod install` isn't executed as part of `flutter run`. This is consistent with the log output at the start of your Logs section.

Can I ask you to remove `example/ios/Podfile.lock` and retry `flutter run` in `health_stats`?

(Meanwhile, I'll be trying to reproduce the error of the `regular` project.)
